# The Six-Tool Pattern: MCP Server Design That Scales > Raw source preserved verbatim. Do not edit this file. > Compiled into wiki articles during the ingest stage. --- The six-tool pattern provides a structured approach to MCP server design that balances AI agent control with context efficiency. Rather than creating 15-20 tools with redundant functionality, this pattern consolidates operations into six purpose-driven tools that minimize token waste while maintaining full capabilities. ## The Six Tools **Category 1: Universal Discovery (OpenAI-Compatible)** 1. **Fetch** — Retrieves specific items using encoded ID strings. The server handles routing complexity internally while the AI uses simple string parameters. 2. **Search** — Accepts query strings supporting both natural language and structured syntax.
